items.title: Amazon early Memorial Day deals from $15: Echo, Ring Pro, Blink, more
items.summary:
Memorial Day 2026 is right around the corner now and the Amazon Devices deals are already going live. Alongside some carry over Amazon lows on its latest Echo Show 8 and Show 11 smart displays, as well as the Echo Dot Max and Echo Studio, it has already added some notable smart home deals into the mix well ahead of this year’s holiday. Firstly, we have quite a rare discount on some of the latest Ring Doorbells, including the 4K Pro model, alongside some ultra-affordable Blink gear from just $15. Scope it all out below and expect plenty more to land in the coming days, and weeks.

items.title: Regularly $1,100 LG UltraGear 34-inch 240Hz OLED ultrawide monitor hits $658 all-time low (40% off)
items.summary:
We are now tracking a deal at Amazon that drops LG’s UltraGear (34GX90SA-W) 34-inch 240Hz WQHD OLED gaming monitor down to $658.45 shipped. Originally priced at $1,400, this ultrawide display has more recently been hovering around the $1,100 mark at Amazon, making this offer a particularly notable one. Today’s deal knocks a massive $442 off its usual going rate, delivering a solid 40% discount and marking the lowest price we’ve tracked for this model so far. While it may not be the cheapest ultrawide gaming monitor on the market, it’s still an excellent value for a curved OLED panel with a 240Hz refresh rate. We recently saw the larger 39-inch version of this monitor hit a new all-time low as well, and now the more compact 34-inch variant is now at a great price for folks looking for a slightly smaller setup. Head below for more details.

items.title: ENGWE’s latest M20 3.0 moped-style e-bike gets $114 bundle for continuing lows from $1,349, more models from $949
items.summary:
ENGWE’s Anniversary Sale may be well past over, but the brand is continuing to offer the three included e-bikes there at their absolute best prices, just with smaller bundle packages of up to $134 in value and no battery vouchers – plus, the bonus savings code we discovered is still active for $50 extra off the price! Of course, the model that has made the biggest waves lately is the newest M20 3.0 Moped-Style e-bike, which has a single-battery configuration for $1,349 shipped, after using the code DEAL50 at checkout, or the dual-battery configuration for $1,649 shipped, after using the same code. Both also come with $114 bundles (a rear rack + cargo net). This new e-bike has only been on the market since the beginning of March with $1,999 and $2,499 full prices, which we’ve seen dropped to $1,399 and $1,699 during their initial launch periods, as well as during the previous anniversary sale – which is when we also stumbled upon the bonus savings code that marked their newest low prices. These ongoing savings allow you to continue scoring $650 and $850 markdowns (and $764 and $964 total savings) for the best prices we have tracked. Head below to browse all three continuing e-bike deals.
